Unknown industry \u2192 default to standard tier with neutral language.","input_schema":[{"name":"auto_research_depth","type":"number","default":3,"min":1,"max":10,"description":"Web sources per research target (\u00d75 targets). 1=quick, 3=standard, 5=deep-enterprise."},{"name":"proposal_template","type":"enum","options":["auto","startup","standard","enterprise"],"default":"auto","description":"Template override. 'auto' picks tier from deal value."},{"name":"max_proposals_per_run","type":"number","default":10,"min":1,"max":50,"description":"Max proposals generated per polling cycle. Prevents review-queue overflow."}],"sample_input":{"auto_research_depth":"{{param.aq_research_depth}}","proposal_template":"{{param.aq_template_override}}","max_proposals_per_run":"{{param.aq_max_per_run}}"},"tool_hints":["http_request","web_search","file_read","file_write"],"test_fixtures":[],"use_case_flow":{"nodes":[{"id":"g1","type":"start","label":"Poll tick"},{"id":"g2","type":"connector","label":"Query unprocessed qualified deals","connector":"crm"},{"id":"g3","type":"action","label":"Validate required fields"},{"id":"g4","type":"decision","label":"Data complete?"},{"id":"g5","type":"action","label":"Generate proposal id + select tier"},{"id":"g6","type":"action","label":"Research company / news / industry / competitors / contact"},{"id":"g7","type":"action","label":"Compose six-section proposal"},{"id":"g8","type":"connector","label":"Create knowledge base proposal page","connector":"knowledge_base"},{"id":"g9","type":"action","label":"Flag for manual review"},{"id":"g10","type":"event","label":"Emit sales.proposal.created"},{"id":"g11","type":"event","label":"Emit sales.proposal.deal_incomplete"},{"id":"g12","type":"end","label":"Cycle complete"}],"edges":[{"source":"g1","target":"g2"},{"source":"g2","target":"g3"},{"source":"g3","target":"g4"},{"source":"g4","target":"g5","variant":"yes","label":"Complete"},{"source":"g4","target":"g11","variant":"no","label":"Incomplete"},{"source":"g5","target":"g6"},{"source":"g6","target":"g7"},{"source":"g7","target":"g8"},{"source":"g8","target":"g9"},{"source":"g9","target":"g10"},{"source":"g10","target":"g12"},{"source":"g11","target":"g12"}]}}

recipes.name: uc_review_learning
recipes.description: On review completion, apply approval or revision outcome: approved → memory reinforces successful pattern, status updated in knowledge base, approval event emitted. Revision requested → regenerate only flagged sections, resubmit for review, memory records the revision pattern to prevent recurrence. Event-driven; not user-configurable.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_review_learning","title":"Review Learning","description":"On review completion, apply approval or revision outcome: approved \u2192 memory reinforces successful pattern, status updated in knowledge base, approval event emitted. Revision requested \u2192 regenerate only flagged sections, resubmit for review, memory records the revision pattern to prevent recurrence. Event-driven; not user-configurable.","capability_summary":"Post-review feedback loop that sharpens future proposals.","category":"review","enabled_by_default":true,"execution_mode":"e2e","model_override":null,"suggested_trigger":{"trigger_type":"event_listener","config":{"event_type":"sales.review.completed"},"description":"Fires when a manual review completes. Not user-configurable \u2014 event triggers bypass the composition picker."},"connectors":["knowledge_base"],"notification_channels":[],"review_policy":{"mode":"never","context":null},"memory_policy":{"enabled":true,"context":"Approval patterns (winning framings by industry + tier) and revision patterns (common reviewer complaints) stored for progressive learning. Approval-rate analytics per industry + tier in `proposal_analytics.json`."},"event_subscriptions":[{"event_type":"sales.review.completed","direction":"listen","description":"Listens for review decisions (approved | revision_requested | rejected)."},{"event_type":"sales.proposal.approved","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { proposal_id, company, deal_value, notion_url }. Consumed by delivery automation."},{"event_type":"sales.proposal.revision_needed","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { proposal_id, feedback_summary }. Tracked for pattern learning."}],"error_handling":"knowledge base update failure \u2192 retry once, then emit user_message; proposal state preserved in `proposal_log.json`. Feedback parse failure \u2192 request clarification from reviewer via messaging, don't regenerate blindly.","input_schema":[],"sample_input":{},"tool_hints":["http_request","file_read","file_write"],"test_fixtures":[],"use_case_flow":{"nodes":[{"id":"l1","type":"start","label":"review.completed received"},{"id":"l2","type":"decision","label":"Approved or revision?"},{"id":"l3","type":"connector","label":"Update knowledge base \u2192 approved","connector":"knowledge_base"},{"id":"l4","type":"action","label":"Log winning pattern to memory"},{"id":"l5","type":"event","label":"Emit proposal.approved"},{"id":"l6","type":"action","label":"Parse feedback + regenerate flagged sections"},{"id":"l7","type":"connector","label":"Update knowledge base \u2192 re-review","connector":"knowledge_base"},{"id":"l8","type":"action","label":"Log revision pattern to memory"},{"id":"l9","type":"event","label":"Emit proposal.revision_needed"},{"id":"l10","type":"action","label":"Update proposal_analytics.json"},{"id":"l11","type":"end","label":"Learning cycle complete"}],"edges":[{"source":"l1","target":"l2"},{"source":"l2","target":"l3","variant":"yes","label":"Approved"},{"source":"l2","target":"l6","variant":"no","label":"Revision"},{"source":"l3","target":"l4"},{"source":"l4","target":"l5"},{"source":"l5","target":"l10"},{"source":"l6","target":"l7"},{"source":"l7","target":"l8"},{"source":"l8","target":"l9"},{"source":"l9","target":"l10"},{"source":"l10","target":"l11"}]}}

recipes.name: uc_edit_draft
recipes.description: Receive a scientific draft, apply the 12 Sainani rule categories with per-sentence citations and explanations, deliver a diff + clean rewrite + style report, capture author accept/reject/counter-edit decisions, and learn author voice over time without breaking the underlying rules.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_edit_draft","title":"Sainani-Rule Draft Edit","description":"Receive a scientific draft, apply the 12 Sainani rule categories with per-sentence citations and explanations, deliver a diff + clean rewrite + style report, capture author accept/reject/counter-edit decisions, and learn author voice over time without breaking the underlying rules.","capability_summary":"Rule-cited diff + clean rewrite + style report; learns author voice per rule.","category":"content","enabled_by_default":true,"execution_mode":"e2e","model_override":null,"suggested_trigger":{"trigger_type":"manual","config":{},"description":"Manual \u2014 author pastes text or provides a file path. Default manual \u2014 final cadence set at the trigger-composition step."},"connectors":[],"notification_channels":[],"review_policy":{"mode":"always","context":"Every edit delivery is the review moment. Author responds with accept-all / accept-some / reject / counter-edit. Decisions feed the learning loop \u2014 acceptance counters, voice deltas, jargon exemptions."},"memory_policy":{"enabled":true,"context":"Per-rule acceptance rate per author, domain vocabulary (terms not to flag as jargon), common rejection patterns, counter-edit voice deltas. After 20+ edits per rule, voice notes surface: 'this author accepts R7 95% of the time \u2014 safe to apply with minimal explanation'."},"event_subscriptions":[{"event_type":"scientific_editor.draft.reviewed","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: document_type, word_count, edit_count, top_rules[], draft_number. Emitted after diff + rewrite + report are delivered."},{"event_type":"scientific_editor.changes.accepted","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: acceptance_rate, accepted_count, rejected_count, counter_edit_count. Emitted after the author responds."},{"event_type":"scientific_editor.voice.learned","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: rule_id, insight, edit_count. Emitted periodically when 20+ edits surface a new voice insight for this author."}],"error_handling":"Too short (<50 words) -> apply rules, note that more text helps. Too long (>10k) -> suggest split, process first 5k. Non-English -> decline. Zero suggestions -> positive style report. Conflicting rules -> pick highest impact. First-run -> default confidence, note adaptation curve. Content edit requested -> decline, offer to flag under-supported claims.","input_schema":[{"name":"draft_text","type":"text","description":"The draft to edit. Paste text, a file path, or a section reference."},{"name":"document_type","type":"enum","options":["abstract","introduction","methods","results","discussion","general"],"default":"general","description":"Document type. Abstracts get strictest rules; methods allow some passive voice."},{"name":"strictness","type":"enum","options":["strict","standard","lenient"],"default":"standard","description":"Strict flags everything for learning signal; standard is balanced; lenient surfaces only highest-impact edits."}],"sample_input":{"draft_text":"","document_type":"general","strictness":"{{param.aq_default_strictness}}"},"tool_hints":["file_read","file_write"],"test_fixtures":[],"use_case_flow":{"nodes":[{"id":"e1","type":"start","label":"Manual: draft submitted"},{"id":"e2","type":"action","label":"Detect document type + load author memory"},{"id":"e3","type":"action","label":"Pre-scan (mechanical flags)"},{"id":"e4","type":"action","label":"Apply Sainani rules with memory filtering"},{"id":"e5","type":"action","label":"Build diff + rewrite + style report"},{"id":"e6","type":"action","label":"Deliver via Messages"},{"id":"e7","type":"decision","label":"Author response (accept / reject / counter)"},{"id":"e8","type":"action","label":"Update author voice memory"},{"id":"e9","type":"event","label":"Emit changes.accepted + voice.learned (if threshold)"},{"id":"e10","type":"end","label":"Edit cycle complete"}],"edges":[{"source":"e1","target":"e2"},{"source":"e2","target":"e3"},{"source":"e3","target":"e4"},{"source":"e4","target":"e5"},{"source":"e5","target":"e6"},{"source":"e6","target":"e7"},{"source":"e7","target":"e8"},{"source":"e8","target":"e9"},{"source":"e9","target":"e10"}]}}

recipes.name: uc_realtime_scan
recipes.description: Webhook-triggered on push events to primary branches. Parse payload, trigger security scanner rescan, poll for completion, retrieve vulnerabilities, dedupe against open Jira tickets, create severity-mapped tickets for new findings, emit regression events for re-introduced closed CVEs, and post severity-routed messaging alerts. Memory suppresses same-CVE messaging within 7 days.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_realtime_scan","title":"Realtime Push Scan","description":"Webhook-triggered on push events to primary branches. Parse payload, trigger security scanner rescan, poll for completion, retrieve vulnerabilities, dedupe against open Jira tickets, create severity-mapped tickets for new findings, emit regression events for re-introduced closed CVEs, and post severity-routed messaging alerts. Memory suppresses same-CVE messaging within 7 days.","capability_summary":"Push-triggered security scanner scan with Jira dedup, ticket creation, and severity-routed messaging alerts.","category":"realtime","enabled_by_default":true,"execution_mode":"e2e","model_override":null,"suggested_trigger":{"trigger_type":"webhook","config":{"event":"push","secret_header":"X-Hub-Signature-256","branches":["main","master"]},"description":"Fires on every push to primary branches. Not a scheduled cadence \u2014 webhook triggers bypass the composition picker."},"connectors":["source_control","security","ticketing","messaging"],"notification_channels":[],"review_policy":{"mode":"on_low_confidence","context":"Critical findings can optionally require a human acknowledgment reaction in the messaging channel before Jira ticket creation \u2014 configurable via aq_critical_gate. Regular findings proceed automatically."},"memory_policy":{"enabled":true,"context":"7-day messaging suppression per CVE (`notified-vulns.json`). Repo \u2192 security scanner project ID mapping. Failed ticket retry queue. Prune entries > 90 days."},"event_subscriptions":[{"event_type":"security.vuln.detected","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { cve_id, severity, cvss_score, package, version, repo, commit_sha, jira_key, snyk_url }. Emitted for NEW findings. Consumed by metric aggregators and downstream remediation workflows."},{"event_type":"security.vuln.tracked","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { cve_id, existing_jira_key, repo }. Emitted when an existing Jira ticket already covers the finding (no new ticket created)."},{"event_type":"security.vuln.regression","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { cve_id, closed_jira_key, re_introduction_commit, cvss, repo }. Consumed by uc_regression_handling."}],"error_handling":"security scanner scan timeout \u2192 log, post warning to the messaging channel, create manual-investigation Jira task, continue. Source-control rate limit \u2192 honor reset header. Jira 401/403 \u2192 user_message event for credential refresh; skip cycle. Jira creation failure \u2192 append to failed-tickets.json for manual retry. messaging failure \u2192 unsent-alerts.txt + user_message for critical vulns.","input_schema":[{"name":"repo_scope","type":"enum","options":["all_org_repos","topic_tagged","explicit_list","existing_snyk_projects"],"default":"topic_tagged","description":"Which repositories the pipeline monitors. Topic-tagged is usually the right balance."},{"name":"min_severity_for_ticket","type":"enum","options":["critical_only","high_and_above","medium_and_above","all_severities"],"default":"high_and_above","description":"Minimum severity that creates a Jira ticket automatically."},{"name":"critical_gate","type":"enum","options":["none","slack_ack","oncall_page","security_lead_approval"],"default":"slack_ack","description":"Human gate before the persona creates a Jira ticket for a Critical finding."},{"name":"auto_resolve_disappeared","type":"enum","options":["never","comment_only","auto_close_if_medium_or_lower"],"default":"comment_only","description":"Behavior when a vuln disappears from a subsequent scan \u2014 the regression-safe default is to comment and let a human close."}],"sample_input":{"repo_scope":"{{param.aq_repo_scope}}","min_severity_for_ticket":"{{param.aq_min_severity_for_ticket}}","critical_gate":"{{param.aq_critical_gate}}","auto_resolve_disappeared":"{{param.aq_auto_resolve_disappeared}}"},"tool_hints":["http_request","file_read","file_write"],"test_fixtures":[],"use_case_flow":{"nodes":[{"id":"r1","type":"start","label":"Push webhook (primary branch)"},{"id":"r2","type":"connector","label":"Trigger security scanner rescan + poll","connector":"snyk"},{"id":"r3","type":"connector","label":"Fetch vulnerabilities","connector":"snyk"},{"id":"r4","type":"connector","label":"JQL dedup query (per CVE)","connector":"ticketing"},{"id":"r5","type":"decision","label":"NEW / EXISTING_OPEN / REGRESSION?"},{"id":"r6","type":"connector","label":"Create severity-mapped Jira ticket","connector":"ticketing"},{"id":"r7","type":"event","label":"Emit security.vuln.detected / tracked / regression"},{"id":"r8","type":"connector","label":"Post severity-routed messaging alert","connector":"messaging"},{"id":"r9","type":"end","label":"Push scan complete"}],"edges":[{"source":"r1","target":"r2"},{"source":"r2","target":"r3"},{"source":"r3","target":"r4"},{"source":"r4","target":"r5"},{"source":"r5","target":"r6","variant":"yes","label":"NEW"},{"source":"r5","target":"r7","variant":"no","label":"Tracked / Regression"},{"source":"r6","target":"r7"},{"source":"r7","target":"r8"},{"source":"r8","target":"r9"}]}}

recipes.name: uc_regression_handling
recipes.description: Event-listens on `security.vuln.regression`. Fetches the closed Jira ticket, retrieves workflow transition IDs, reopens with the 'Reopen' transition, and adds a comment with re-introduction commit SHA, current scan date, CVSS, and the previous fix that was reverted. Always posts a #security-alerts @here message regardless of CVSS — regressions are louder than first findings.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_regression_handling","title":"Regression Handling","description":"Event-listens on `security.vuln.regression`. Fetches the closed Jira ticket, retrieves workflow transition IDs, reopens with the 'Reopen' transition, and adds a comment with re-introduction commit SHA, current scan date, CVSS, and the previous fix that was reverted. Always posts a #security-alerts @here message regardless of CVSS \u2014 regressions are louder than first findings.","capability_summary":"Reopen closed Jira tickets on CVE re-introduction and alert loudly regardless of severity.","category":"response","enabled_by_default":true,"execution_mode":"e2e","model_override":null,"suggested_trigger":{"trigger_type":"event_listener","config":{"event_type":"security.vuln.regression"},"description":"Fires whenever a realtime or scheduled scan detects a re-introduced closed CVE. Not user-configurable \u2014 event triggers bypass the composition picker."},"connectors":["ticketing","messaging"],"notification_channels":[],"review_policy":{"mode":"always","context":"Regression alerts ping humans via @here and emit a user_message event \u2014 a closed CVE resurfacing warrants eyes on it."},"memory_policy":{"enabled":true,"context":"Per-CVE regression count (recurring regressions indicate brittle fixes). Jira transition ID cache per project workflow."},"event_subscriptions":[{"event_type":"security.vuln.regression","direction":"listen","description":"Incoming regression from uc_realtime_scan or uc_scheduled_scan."},{"event_type":"security.vuln.regression_resolved","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { cve_id, jira_key, days_active, remediation_commit }. Emitted when a subsequent scan confirms the re-introduced vuln has been re-fixed."}],"error_handling":"Jira reopen transition failed \u2192 log, write to failed-tickets.json, post messages asking team to manually reopen. Transition ID unknown \u2192 query /transitions once, cache for the project. Jira 401/403 \u2192 user_message event.","input_schema":[],"sample_input":{},"tool_hints":["http_request","file_read","file_write"],"test_fixtures":[],"use_case_flow":{"nodes":[{"id":"g1","type":"start","label":"security.vuln.regression received"},{"id":"g2","type":"connector","label":"Fetch closed Jira ticket + transitions","connector":"ticketing"},{"id":"g3","type":"connector","label":"Reopen ticket + add regression comment","connector":"ticketing"},{"id":"g4","type":"connector","label":"Post @here alert to #security-alerts","connector":"messaging"},{"id":"g5","type":"action","label":"Update memory (increment regression_count)"},{"id":"g6","type":"end","label":"Regression handled"}],"edges":[{"source":"g1","target":"g2"},{"source":"g2","target":"g3"},{"source":"g3","target":"g4"},{"source":"g4","target":"g5"},{"source":"g5","target":"g6"}]}}

recipes.name: uc_scheduled_scan
recipes.description: Periodic sweep of all monitored repositories — catches newly published CVEs affecting existing dependencies without requiring a new commit. Aggregates findings across the org, bulk-deduplicates against open Jira tickets, batch-creates tickets for new findings, emits regression events for re-introduced closed CVEs, and posts a digest to the messaging channel. Default weekly Monday 09:00 + incremental every 6 hours — final cadence set at the trigger-composition step.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_scheduled_scan","title":"Scheduled Full Scan","description":"Periodic sweep of all monitored repositories \u2014 catches newly published CVEs affecting existing dependencies without requiring a new commit. Aggregates findings across the org, bulk-deduplicates against open Jira tickets, batch-creates tickets for new findings, emits regression events for re-introduced closed CVEs, and posts a digest to the messaging channel. Default weekly Monday 09:00 + incremental every 6 hours \u2014 final cadence set at the trigger-composition step.","capability_summary":"Full-org security scanner sweep with bulk Jira dedup, batch ticket creation, and a single digest message.","category":"scheduled","enabled_by_default":true,"execution_mode":"e2e","model_override":null,"suggested_trigger":{"trigger_type":"schedule","config":{"cron":"0 9 * * 1","timezone":"local"},"description":"Default weekly Monday 9:00 AM (plus incremental every 6 hours) \u2014 final cadence set at the trigger-composition step."},"connectors":["source_control","security","ticketing","messaging"],"notification_channels":[],"review_policy":{"mode":"never","context":null},"memory_policy":{"enabled":true,"context":"Shared with uc_realtime_scan \u2014 notified-vulns ledger, repo-project-map, failed-tickets queue."},"event_subscriptions":[{"event_type":"security.scan.digest_delivered","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { scan_date, repos_scanned, new_vulns_by_severity, regressions_count, already_tracked_count }. Consumed by weekly security-ops reports."},{"event_type":"security.vuln.regression","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { cve_id, closed_jira_key, re_introduction_commit, cvss, repo }. Consumed by uc_regression_handling."}],"error_handling":"Source-control rate limit \u2192 honor reset, resume. security scanner timeout on a repo \u2192 skip, note in digest, continue others. Jira bulk-create partial failure \u2192 continue with remaining, queue failed in failed-tickets.json. messaging digest failure \u2192 write to unsent-alerts.txt.","input_schema":[{"name":"compliance_framework","type":"enum","options":["soc2","pci_dss","hipaa_hitrust","iso_27001","internal_only"],"default":"internal_only","description":"Compliance framework that drives Jira due-date SLAs and priority labels."},{"name":"ticket_detail_level","type":"enum","options":["minimal","standard","comprehensive"],"default":"standard","description":"How much context to include in Jira descriptions. Comprehensive works best for teams without a dedicated security engineer."}],"sample_input":{"compliance_framework":"{{param.aq_compliance_framework}}","ticket_detail_level":"{{param.aq_ticket_detail_level}}"},"tool_hints":["http_request","file_read","file_write"],"test_fixtures":[],"use_case_flow":{"nodes":[{"id":"w1","type":"start","label":"Scheduled tick"},{"id":"w2","type":"connector","label":"List monitored repos","connector":"source_control"},{"id":"w3","type":"connector","label":"Sequential security scanner scans (rate-limit aware)","connector":"snyk"},{"id":"w4","type":"connector","label":"Bulk JQL dedup","connector":"ticketing"},{"id":"w5","type":"action","label":"Classify NEW / EXISTING / REGRESSION"},{"id":"w6","type":"connector","label":"Batch-create Jira tickets for NEW","connector":"ticketing"},{"id":"w7","type":"event","label":"Emit regression events for REGRESSION"},{"id":"w8","type":"connector","label":"Post digest to the messaging channel","connector":"messaging"},{"id":"w9","type":"event","label":"Emit security.scan.digest_delivered"},{"id":"w10","type":"end","label":"Full scan complete"}],"edges":[{"source":"w1","target":"w2"},{"source":"w2","target":"w3"},{"source":"w3","target":"w4"},{"source":"w4","target":"w5"},{"source":"w5","target":"w6"},{"source":"w6","target":"w7"},{"source":"w7","target":"w8"},{"source":"w8","target":"w9"},{"source":"w9","target":"w10"}]}}

recipes.name: uc_daily_flush
recipes.description: Scheduled capability. Invokes the platform's `compile_persona_memories` IPC to synthesize raw memories into wiki articles (each backed by ≥2 source memories). Default 02:00 local — final cadence set at the trigger-composition step. Runs at most once per day per persona.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_daily_flush","title":"Daily Memory Flush","description":"Scheduled capability. Invokes the platform's `compile_persona_memories` IPC to synthesize raw memories into wiki articles (each backed by \u22652 source memories). Default 02:00 local \u2014 final cadence set at the trigger-composition step. Runs at most once per day per persona.","capability_summary":"Daily compile pass \u2014 raw memories \u2192 compiled wiki articles via platform IPC.","category":"development","enabled_by_default":true,"execution_mode":"e2e","model_override":null,"suggested_trigger":{"trigger_type":"schedule","config":{"cron":"0 2 * * *"},"description":"Default daily 02:00 local \u2014 final cadence set at the trigger-composition step."},"connectors":[],"notification_channels":[{"type":"messaging","description":"In-app notification when the flush creates \u22651 article. Silent on 0-result."}],"review_policy":{"mode":"never","context":null},"memory_policy":{"enabled":true,"context":"Writes compiled + wiki-tagged articles via the IPC. Writes a flush_event record for each successful compile for audit trails."},"event_subscriptions":[{"event_type":"memory.codebase.compiled","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { source_count, created, created_ids[], titles[] }. Emitted after a successful daily flush with > 0 articles."}],"error_handling":"Insufficient source material (created == 0) \u2192 suppress user_message, retry next cycle. CLI timeout \u2192 log as manual_review item, no auto-retry. Concurrent flush (marker present) \u2192 skip this cycle.","input_schema":[{"name":"compile_source_limit","type":"number","default":50,"min":5,"max":200,"description":"Maximum memories fed into each compile pass. recipes.name: uc_session_capture
recipes.description: Event-listen on persona.execution.completed. Drains the hooks sidecar queue for this persona's exec_dir, extracts decisions / gotchas / follow-ups / file references from each referenced transcript, and stores them as raw-tagged agent memories. Exit silently on empty queue. Event-driven — not configurable at the trigger-composition step.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_session_capture","title":"Session Capture","description":"Event-listen on persona.execution.completed. Drains the hooks sidecar queue for this persona's exec_dir, extracts decisions / gotchas / follow-ups / file references from each referenced transcript, and stores them as raw-tagged agent memories. Exit silently on empty queue. recipes.name: uc_weekly_health
recipes.description: Scheduled capability. Audits the persona's memory layer for stale articles, contradictory pairs, and coverage gaps. Optionally also lints the Obsidian vault via obsidian_brain_lint_vault when configured. Surfaces issues via manual_review — never auto-deletes. Default Monday 09:00 — final cadence set at the trigger-composition step.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_weekly_health","title":"Weekly Memory Health Check","description":"Scheduled capability. Audits the persona's memory layer for stale articles, contradictory pairs, and coverage gaps. Optionally also lints the Obsidian vault via obsidian_brain_lint_vault when configured. recipes.name: uc_issue_scan
recipes.description: Queries Sentry for unresolved issues in scoped projects over the lookback window, filters by severity threshold, applies memory to auto-deprioritize known-noise patterns, and emits qualified issues to the root-cause capability. Default daily 8 AM — final cadence set at the trigger-composition step.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_issue_scan","title":"Sentry Issue Scan","description":"Queries Sentry for unresolved issues in scoped projects over the lookback window, filters by severity threshold, applies memory to auto-deprioritize known-noise patterns, and emits qualified issues to the root-cause capability. Default daily 8 AM \u2014 final cadence set at the trigger-composition step.","capability_summary":"Fetch unresolved Sentry issues with memory-aware noise filtering.","category":"monitoring","enabled_by_default":true,"execution_mode":"e2e","model_override":null,"suggested_trigger":{"trigger_type":"schedule","config":{"cron":"0 8 * * *","timezone":"local"},"description":"Default daily 8 AM \u2014 final cadence set at the trigger-composition step."},"connectors":["sentry"],"notification_channels":[],"review_policy":{"mode":"never","context":null},"memory_policy":{"enabled":true,"context":"`triage_patterns.json` \u2014 per-pattern accept/reject counts. 3+ noise dismissals suppress analysis for that pattern."},"event_subscriptions":[{"event_type":"sentry.issue.qualified","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { sentry_issue_id, shortId, title, culprit, count, userCount, firstSeen, lastSeen, level, platform, project_slug }. Consumed by uc_root_cause."}],"error_handling":"Sentry 401/403 \u2192 cannot proceed, post re-auth alert. 429 \u2192 backoff + truncate to top-N. Below = skipped."},{"name":"lookback_hours","type":"number","default":24,"min":1,"max":168,"description":"Scan window in hours. recipes.name: uc_root_cause
recipes.description: For each qualified issue, fetches the latest event for the full stack trace, reads source code at the error location across scoped codebases, traces cross-project callers, classifies root cause, and composes a Jira-format report with 3 ranked resolutions. One message per issue; each routes through human triage. Fires on `sentry.issue.qualified` events.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_root_cause","title":"Codebase Root Cause Analysis","description":"For each qualified issue, fetches the latest event for the full stack trace, reads source code at the error location across scoped codebases, traces cross-project callers, classifies root cause, and composes a Jira-format report with 3 ranked resolutions. One message per issue; each routes through human triage. Fires on `sentry.issue.qualified` events.","capability_summary":"Stack-trace-grounded root cause report with 3 ranked resolutions per issue.","category":"analysis","enabled_by_default":true,"execution_mode":"e2e","model_override":null,"suggested_trigger":{"trigger_type":"event_listener","config":{"event_type":"sentry.issue.qualified"},"description":"Fires whenever uc_issue_scan emits a qualified issue. Not user-configurable \u2014 event triggers bypass the composition picker."},"connectors":["sentry","codebases"],"notification_channels":[],"review_policy":{"mode":"always","context":"Each issue report presents for human triage. Actionable \u2192 user selects resolution + notes; Noise \u2192 user logs reason. Triage decisions persist in memory and shape future noise filtering."},"memory_policy":{"enabled":true,"context":"`triage_patterns.json` \u2014 error pattern signature \u2192 decision (actionable/noise) + user reasoning + problem type. Builds a problem type taxonomy over time."},"event_subscriptions":[{"event_type":"sentry.issue.qualified","direction":"listen","description":"Qualified issue from uc_issue_scan \u2014 enters root-cause analysis."},{"event_type":"sentry.issue.critical","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { sentry_issue_id, shortId, severity: 'Critical', user_count, count }. Emitted for any issue classified Critical."},{"event_type":"sentry.backlog.item_added","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { sentry_issue_id, selected_resolution_tier: 'quick'|'proper'|'systemic', user_notes }. Emitted on human Actionable decision."},{"event_type":"sentry.daily.summary","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { run_date, total_scanned, total_surfaced, severity_distribution, trend }. Emitted once per scan run."}],"error_handling":"Codebases connector offline \u2192 simplified Sentry-data-only report with note. File not in any scoped codebase \u2192 simplified report with raw trace. Minified code \u2192 include location + flag source maps. No stack trace \u2192 simplified report (description + frequency only). Cross-project search returns too many results \u2192 focus on top frame + project matching Sentry slug.","input_schema":[{"name":"report_depth","type":"enum","options":["summary","standard","deep"],"default":"standard","description":"Summary: title + severity + one-line root cause. Standard: full Jira-format. recipes.name: uc_daily_digest

recipes.name: uc_inventory_watch
recipes.description: Fetch inventory levels per variant, compare against warn / critical thresholds, calculate sell-through velocity, and alert on new threshold breaches. Deduplicate so the same tier doesn't re-alert. Default every minute — final cadence set at the trigger-composition step.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_inventory_watch","title":"Inventory Watch","description":"Fetch inventory levels per variant, compare against warn / critical thresholds, calculate sell-through velocity, and alert on new threshold breaches. recipes.name: uc_issue_writer
recipes.description: Event-driven capability. Subscribes to `review_decision.approved` (filtered to this persona's own reviews). Fetches the review's context_data via IPC callback, builds a unified diff, and POSTs an issue to GitHub or GitLab (selected by issue_platform) with labels and a `Skill-Ref: <slug>` footer. Emits `skill.update.issue_created` on success.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_issue_writer","title":"Skill-Update Issue Writer","description":"Event-driven capability. Subscribes to `review_decision.approved` (filtered to this persona's own reviews). Fetches the review's context_data via IPC callback, builds a unified diff, and POSTs an issue to GitHub or GitLab (selected by issue_platform) with labels and a `Skill-Ref: <slug>` footer. Emits `skill.update.issue_created` on success.","capability_summary":"On approve \u2192 fetch review body \u2192 file GitHub/GitLab issue with diff and slug marker.","category":"development","enabled_by_default":true,"execution_mode":"e2e","model_override":null,"suggested_trigger":{"trigger_type":"event_listener","config":{"event_type":"review_decision.approved"},"description":"Fires when the user approves a manual_review surfaced by this persona. Suppressed when issue_platform: none (memory-only mode)."}],"review_policy":{"mode":"never","context":null},"memory_policy":{"enabled":true,"context":"Saves an `[approved, skill-librarian]` memory per issue with slug + reviewer_notes \u2014 Phase-2 learning that shapes future UC1 proposals."},"event_subscriptions":[{"event_type":"review_decision.approved","direction":"listen","description":"Triggers UC2. recipes.name: uc_skill_audit
recipes.description: Scheduled + manual capability. Diffs codebase since last_scan_at, reads every `.claude/skills/<slug>/SKILL.md`, computes per-slug usage from `Skill-Ref:` commit markers, and surfaces up to `max_proposals_per_run` edit or retirement proposals as manual_review items with full diffs in context_data. Default cadence Monday 09:00 local — final cadence set at the trigger-composition step.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_skill_audit","title":"Skill Audit & Proposal","description":"Scheduled + manual capability. Diffs codebase since last_scan_at, reads every `.claude/skills/<slug>/SKILL.md`, computes per-slug usage from `Skill-Ref:` commit markers, and surfaces up to `max_proposals_per_run` edit or retirement proposals as manual_review items with full diffs in context_data. Also runs on manual invocation from the persona detail page."},"connectors":["codebase"],"notification_channels":[{"type":"messaging","description":"Single audit summary per run listing proposal counts and a review queue link. Silent on zero proposals."}],"review_policy":{"mode":"always","context":"Every edit / retirement proposal requires human approval. Rejected proposals land in rejected_patterns for a 30-day cooldown."},"memory_policy":{"enabled":true,"context":"Writes `last_scan_at` marker and (slug, change_signature) rejection records. recipes.name: uc_design_batch
recipes.description: Transform a topic brief into a batch of branded social media variations. Loads the Canva brand kit, generates platform-optimized copy, creates multiple variations (autofill brand template when matching, from-scratch otherwise), delivers previews + edit links for review, and learns which approaches the user selects.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_design_batch","title":"Branded Design Batch","description":"Transform a topic brief into a batch of branded social media variations. Loads the Canva brand kit, generates platform-optimized copy, creates multiple variations (autofill brand template when matching, from-scratch otherwise), delivers previews + edit links for review, and learns which approaches the user selects.","capability_summary":"Brand kit + platform-tuned copy + multiple variations + selection-driven learning.","category":"content","enabled_by_default":true,"execution_mode":"e2e","model_override":null,"suggested_trigger":{"trigger_type":"manual","config":{},"description":"Manual \u2014 user provides topic + platform + tone. Default manual \u2014 final cadence set at the trigger-composition step."},"connectors":["canva"],"notification_channels":[],"review_policy":{"mode":"always","context":"Each variation is reviewed \u2014 user marks Select (use this one) or Pass (not this one), with optional feedback. Multiple selections allowed for A/B testing. 3+ passes on a style for a platform deprioritizes that approach."},"memory_policy":{"enabled":true,"context":"Selected / passed variation styles per platform, copy tone patterns (questions vs statistics vs statements), layout density preferences (minimal vs information-dense), topic history (last 10), platform-specific approval rates."},"event_subscriptions":[{"event_type":"social_designer.batch.created","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: platform, topic, variation_count, design_ids[]. Emitted when the batch of variations is created and ready for review."},{"event_type":"social_designer.variation.selected","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: design_id, edit_url, platform, topic, variation_label. Emitted per selected variation \u2014 downstream export or publishing agents can subscribe."}],"error_handling":"Canva auth failure -> report + skip. Rate limit -> pause 60s, reduce variations. Topic overlaps recent -> adjust angle.","input_schema":[{"name":"topic","type":"text","description":"The subject matter for the design (e.g., 'Top 5 AI Tools for 2026', 'Weekly productivity tip')."},{"name":"platform","type":"enum","options":["instagram_carousel","instagram_story","linkedin","twitter","youtube_thumbnail","facebook_cover"],"default":"instagram_carousel","description":"Target platform. More variations = more choice but more Canva quota."},{"name":"slide_count","type":"number","default":5,"min":3,"max":15,"description":"Slides for carousel formats (including hook and CTA). recipes.name: uc_export_designs
recipes.description: Export selected Canva designs as downloadable PNG/PDF files. Listens for selection events from uc_design_batch (or runs on manual request) and delivers download links via Messages, with edit URL fallback on export timeout.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_export_designs","title":"Design Export","description":"Export selected Canva designs as downloadable PNG/PDF files. recipes.name: uc_dunning_sequence
recipes.description: Event-driven 3-tier failed-payment recovery. Tier 1 = friendly reminder; tier 2 = urgent warning (+ messaging); tier 3 = final 48-hour warning + 'Churn Imminent' flag + messaging escalation. Resets on mid-sequence payment success; stops immediately on cancellation.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_dunning_sequence","title":"Dunning Sequence","description":"Event-driven 3-tier failed-payment recovery. Tier 1 = friendly reminder; tier 2 = urgent warning (+ messaging); tier 3 = final 48-hour warning + 'Churn Imminent' flag + messaging escalation. Resets on mid-sequence payment success; stops immediately on cancellation.","capability_summary":"3-tier escalating dunning with mid-sequence reversal + cancellation handling.","category":"communication","enabled_by_default":true,"execution_mode":"e2e","model_override":null,"suggested_trigger":{"trigger_type":"event_listener","config":{"event_type":"billing.payment.failed"},"description":"Fires on every payment failure from uc_event_sync. Not user-configurable \u2014 event triggers bypass the composition picker."},"connectors":["finance","spreadsheet","email","messaging"],"notification_channels":[{"type":"email","description":"Tier-appropriate dunning emails with billing-portal CTA."},{"type":"slack","description":"Tier 2 risk alert and tier 3 escalation to #billing-alerts."}],"review_policy":{"mode":"on_low_confidence","context":"Tier 3 (final 48-hour warning) can be set to require human approval before sending via the aq_tier3_approval setting. Tiers 1\u20132 always auto-send."},"memory_policy":{"enabled":true,"context":"`dunning_state.json` \u2014 per-customer tier + timestamps. Churn signal memory (plans + payment methods with high failure rates) feeds future alerting."},"event_subscriptions":[{"event_type":"billing.payment.failed","direction":"listen","description":"Failure incoming from uc_event_sync."},{"event_type":"billing.payment.succeeded","direction":"listen","description":"Success incoming from uc_event_sync \u2014 may reset active dunning state."},{"event_type":"billing.subscription.canceled","direction":"listen","description":"Cancellation incoming \u2014 immediately stops any active dunning."},{"event_type":"billing.dunning.tier_changed","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: customer_id, new_tier (1|2|3). Fires per tier change."},{"event_type":"billing.dunning.recovered","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: customer_id, tier_at_recovery. Fires when a payment succeeds mid-dunning."}],"error_handling":"email send fail for dunning \u2192 critical; retry once; queue locally on second failure; alert messaging #billing-alerts. spreadsheet dunning state write fail \u2192 retry; queue to `pending_airtable_ops.jsonl` on continued failure. Cancellation during dunning \u2192 stop immediately, mark Canceled. Payment success during dunning \u2192 reset tier to 0, restore Active, send success email.","input_schema":[{"name":"tone","type":"enum","options":["warm_empathetic","direct_urgent","neutral_transactional"],"default":"warm_empathetic","description":"Tone of the dunning email copy across all tiers."},{"name":"tier3_approval","type":"enum","options":["auto_send","require_human_approval"],"default":"require_human_approval","description":"Whether tier 3 (final warning) auto-sends or surfaces for owner approval first."}],"sample_input":{"tone":"{{param.aq_tone}}","tier3_approval":"{{param.aq_tier3_approval}}"},"tool_hints":["http_request","gmail_send","file_read","file_write"],"test_fixtures":[],"use_case_flow":{"nodes":[{"id":"d1","type":"start","label":"billing.payment.failed received"},{"id":"d2","type":"connector","label":"Read failure history from spreadsheet","connector":"spreadsheet"},{"id":"d3","type":"decision","label":"Which tier?"},{"id":"d4","type":"connector","label":"Send tier-appropriate email","connector":"email"},{"id":"d5","type":"connector","label":"messaging alert (tier 2+ only)","connector":"messaging"},{"id":"d6","type":"connector","label":"Update spreadsheet dunning state","connector":"spreadsheet"},{"id":"d7","type":"event","label":"Emit billing.dunning.tier_changed"},{"id":"d8","type":"end","label":"Dunning step complete"}],"edges":[{"source":"d1","target":"d2"},{"source":"d2","target":"d3"},{"source":"d3","target":"d4","label":"1 / 2 / 3"},{"source":"d4","target":"d5"},{"source":"d5","target":"d6"},{"source":"d6","target":"d7"},{"source":"d7","target":"d8"}]}}

recipes.name: uc_event_sync
recipes.description: Processes finance platform webhook events (subscription + transaction) in real time. Dedupes, classifies, and reconciles spreadsheet. Posts contextual messaging notifications (celebrations to #revenue, failures to #billing-alerts). Fires whenever a finance platform webhook arrives — not user-configurable at the cadence level.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_event_sync","title":"finance platform Event Sync","description":"Processes finance platform webhook events (subscription + transaction) in real time. recipes.name: uc_monthly_mrr_report
recipes.description: Pulls active subscriptions from spreadsheet, computes MRR (total, new, churned, expansion, net), compares to last month, detects anomalies (churn >5%, MRR drop >10%). Writes CSV backup locally, posts a summary to the messaging channel #revenue, and emails the detailed HTML report to the finance team. Default 1st of month 09:00 — final cadence set at the trigger-composition step.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_monthly_mrr_report","title":"Monthly MRR Report","description":"Pulls active subscriptions from spreadsheet, computes MRR (total, new, churned, expansion, net), compares to last month, detects anomalies (churn >5%, MRR drop >10%). Default 1st of month 09:00 \u2014 final cadence set at the trigger-composition step.","capability_summary":"Monthly MRR waterfall + anomaly detection + multi-channel distribution.","category":"reporting","enabled_by_default":true,"execution_mode":"e2e","model_override":null,"suggested_trigger":{"trigger_type":"schedule","config":{"cron":"0 9 1 * *","timezone":"local"},"description":"Default 1st of month 09:00 \u2014 final cadence set at the trigger-composition step."},"connectors":["spreadsheet","messaging","email"],"notification_channels":[{"type":"slack","description":"Summary posted to #revenue."},{"type":"email","description":"Detailed HTML report to finance team recipients."}],"review_policy":{"mode":"never","context":null},"memory_policy":{"enabled":true,"context":"CSV snapshot per month (`data/mrr_reports/{YYYY-MM}.csv`) used as the base for next month's comparisons and anomaly thresholds."},"event_subscriptions":[{"event_type":"billing.report.monthly_delivered","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: month, total_mrr, new_mrr, churned_mrr, expansion_mrr, net_delta. recipes.name: uc_email_triage
recipes.description: Fetches unread email support emails, classifies priority using multi-signal analysis (keywords, sender history, impact language, sender frequency), creates structured knowledge base tickets with priority-tier SLA deadlines, and marks email as read on success. Default every 3 minutes — final cadence set at the trigger-composition step.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_email_triage","title":"Email Triage & Ticketing","description":"Fetches unread email support emails, classifies priority using multi-signal analysis (keywords, sender history, impact language, sender frequency), creates structured knowledge base tickets with priority-tier SLA deadlines, and marks email as read on success. recipes.name: uc_sla_monitor
recipes.description: Queries knowledge base for open tickets approaching or past their SLA deadline and escalates through warning (80%), critical (90%, unassigned), and breach (100%) stages. Default every 3 minutes (paired with triage) — final cadence set at the trigger-composition step.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_sla_monitor","title":"SLA Monitoring & Escalation","description":"Queries knowledge base for open tickets approaching or past their SLA deadline and escalates through warning (80%), critical (90%, unassigned), and breach (100%) stages. recipes.name: uc_daily_digest

recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_daily_digest","title":"Daily Escalation Digest","description":"Queries knowledge base for today's escalations plus 7-day history, compiles stats (counts, tier breakdown, top offenders, avg breach duration, WoW trend), and emails a styled HTML digest to the support lead. recipes.name: uc_sla_watch
recipes.description: Polls support platform for tickets approaching or past SLA, classifies severity by tier, enriches with repeat-offender + agent-overload patterns, posts formatted messaging layout escalations to the messaging channel (DM to lead on Critical), and logs every escalation in knowledge base. Default every 2 minutes — final cadence set at the trigger-composition step.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_sla_watch","title":"SLA Breach Watch & Escalation","description":"Polls support platform for tickets approaching or past SLA, classifies severity by tier, enriches with repeat-offender + agent-overload patterns, posts formatted messaging layout escalations to the messaging channel (DM to lead on Critical), and logs every escalation in knowledge base. recipes.description: On ticket resolution, extracts problem + root_cause + resolution_steps, updates the matching knowledge base KB entry (increment Frequency, update Last Used) or creates a new one. Event-listens on support platform ticket status transitioning to solved/closed.

recipes.name: uc_ticket_triage
recipes.description: Polls support platform for new/updated tickets, classifies sentiment + urgency, searches the knowledge base KB for matching resolutions, and routes per the urgency × KB-match matrix. Default every 60 seconds — final cadence set at the trigger-composition step.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_ticket_triage","title":"Ticket Triage & Routing","description":"Polls support platform for new/updated tickets, classifies sentiment + urgency, searches the knowledge base KB for matching resolutions, and routes per the urgency \u00d7 KB-match matrix. recipes.description: Queries the database for new responses, validates and normalizes, runs sentiment + theme + NPS analysis, compares against rolling baselines, and flags anomalies. Default daily 09:00 — final cadence set at the trigger-composition step.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_process_and_analyze","title":"Process and Analyze","description":"Queries the database for new responses, validates and normalizes, runs sentiment + theme + NPS analysis, compares against rolling baselines, and flags anomalies. recipes.name: uc_process_responses
recipes.description: Polls the Google Form's linked sheet, scores each new response, writes enriched records to spreadsheet, and broadcasts tier-aware summaries to the messaging channel (routing detractors to a support channel). Default polling every 2 minutes — final cadence set at the trigger-composition step.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_process_responses","title":"Process Responses","description":"Polls the Google Form's linked sheet, scores each new response, writes enriched records to spreadsheet, and broadcasts tier-aware summaries to the messaging channel (routing detractors to a support channel). Default polling every 2 minutes \u2014 final cadence set at the trigger-composition step.","capability_summary":"spreadsheet poll + scoring + spreadsheet write + messaging broadcast.","category":"automation","enabled_by_default":true,"execution_mode":"e2e","model_override":null,"suggested_trigger":{"trigger_type":"polling","config":{"interval_seconds":120},"description":"Default every 2 minutes \u2014 final cadence set at the trigger-composition step."},"connectors":["spreadsheet","messaging"],"notification_channels":[{"type":"messaging","description":"messaging summaries + detractor tagging."}],"review_policy":{"mode":"on_low_confidence","context":"Detractor responses optionally gate through manual review before escalation and email, per adoption choice."},"memory_policy":{"enabled":true,"context":"Last processed row index + airtable_records (row_index \u2192 record_id) for dedup and audit."},"event_subscriptions":[{"event_type":"survey.response.processed","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { row_index, respondent, tier, score, airtable_record_id }. Consumed by uc_send_followup_email."},{"event_type":"survey.response.detractor","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { row_index, respondent, flags, escalation_urgency }. Downstream alert pipelines can subscribe."}],"error_handling":"See persona error_handling. Critical: retry queue for spreadsheet writes; never lose a processed row.","input_schema":[{"name":"rubric","type":"enum","options":["nps_promoter_passive_detractor","interest_high_medium_low","sentiment_positive_neutral_negative","custom_defined_at_setup"],"default":"nps_promoter_passive_detractor","description":"Scoring rubric applied to each response."},{"name":"slack_channel","type":"text","description":"Primary messaging channels for response summaries."},{"name":"detractor_gate","type":"enum","options":["all_detractors_human_review","urgency_keywords_only","high_value_accounts_only","fully_automate"],"default":"all_detractors_human_review","description":"When to pause the pipeline and require human review for low-score tick"},{"id":"p2","type":"connector","label":"Fetch Sheet rows","connector":"spreadsheet"},{"id":"p3","type":"action","label":"Slice > last_processed_row"},{"id":"p4","type":"action","label":"Score + classify tier + flags"},{"id":"p5","type":"connector","label":"Create spreadsheet record","connector":"spreadsheet"},{"id":"p6","type":"connector","label":"Post messaging summary","connector":"messaging"},{"id":"p7","type":"decision","label":"Detractor or urgency flag?"},{"id":"p8","type":"connector","label":"Tag support-urgent channel","connector":"messaging"},{"id":"p9","type":"event","label":"Emit survey.response.processed (+ detractor if applicable)"},{"id":"p10","type":"action","label":"Update last_processed_row"},{"id":"p11","type":"end","label":"Cycle complete"}],"edges":[{"source":"p1","target":"p2"},{"source":"p2","target":"p3"},{"source":"p3","target":"p4"},{"source":"p4","target":"p5"},{"source":"p5","target":"p6"},{"source":"p6","target":"p7"},{"source":"p7","target":"p8","label":"Yes","variant":"yes"},{"source":"p7","target":"p9","label":"No","variant":"no"},{"source":"p8","target":"p9"},{"source":"p9","target":"p10"},{"source":"p10","target":"p11"}]}}

recipes.name: uc_send_followup_email
recipes.description: Consumes processed responses and sends the tier-appropriate follow-up email (Promoter / Neutral / Detractor). Failed sends queue for retry.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_send_followup_email","title":"Send Follow-up Email","description":"Consumes processed responses and sends the tier-appropriate follow-up email (Promoter / Neutral / Detractor). Failed sends queue for retry.","capability_summary":"Tier-adaptive follow-up email with retry queue.","category":"automation","enabled_by_default":true,"execution_mode":"e2e","model_override":null,"suggested_trigger":{"trigger_type":"event_listener","config":{"event_type":"survey.response.processed"},"description":"Fires per processed response. recipes.name: uc_code_linking
recipes.description: After acceptance, searches the chosen source-control provider for PRs and commits mentioning the ADR, presents candidate links for confirmation, and writes confirmed references into the knowledge base pages. Cross-references related or superseded ADRs.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_code_linking","title":"Code Linking","description":"After acceptance, searches the chosen source-control provider for PRs and commits mentioning the ADR, presents candidate links for confirmation, and writes confirmed references into the knowledge base pages. Cross-references related or superseded ADRs.","capability_summary":"PR / commit discovery + confirmation + Related Code + Related ADRs wiring.","category":"automation","enabled_by_default":true,"execution_mode":"e2e","model_override":null,"suggested_trigger":{"trigger_type":"event_listener","config":{"event_type":"adr.decision.accepted"},"description":"Fires whenever an ADR reaches Accepted. Not user-configurable \u2014 event triggers bypass the trigger-composition picker."},"connectors":["knowledge_base","source_control"],"notification_channels":[],"review_policy":{"mode":"conditional","context":"Candidate PR matches are presented for manual confirmation before being written to the ADR \u2014 prevents noise."},"memory_policy":{"enabled":true,"context":"Keyword watch list \u2014 future PRs referencing accepted ADR numbers are auto-flagged in the next linking pass."},"event_subscriptions":[{"event_type":"adr.decision.accepted","direction":"listen","description":"Incoming trigger from uc_decision_intake when an ADR is accepted."},{"event_type":"adr.decision.linked","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { adr_number, pr_urls[], related_adr_numbers[] }."}],"error_handling":"Source control 401/403 \u2192 skip linking for this ADR, note 'credential needs refresh' in the knowledge base Related Code section. recipes.name: uc_decision_intake
recipes.description: Manual-trigger intake that validates, enriches, and documents a new technical decision as a structured ADR in knowledge base with alternatives analysis and stakeholder sign-off.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_decision_intake","title":"Decision Intake","description":"Manual-trigger intake that validates, enriches, and documents a new technical decision as a structured ADR in knowledge base with alternatives analysis and stakeholder sign-off.","capability_summary":"Full ADR creation pipeline with enrichment interview, alternatives matrix, and sign-off gate.","category":"automation","enabled_by_default":true,"execution_mode":"e2e","model_override":null,"suggested_trigger":{"trigger_type":"manual","config":{},"description":"Manual invocation when a decision is ready to document \u2014 not scheduled. The user kicks off intake with a title, context, and optional alternatives."},"connectors":["knowledge_base"],"notification_channels":[],"review_policy":{"mode":"conditional","context":"Enrichment questions and stakeholder sign-off both run as manual reviews. Sign-off is gated by `aq_require_sign_off`; enrichment is always required when alternatives or rationale are missing."},"memory_policy":{"enabled":true,"context":"Stakeholder participation patterns, typical decision categories, and enrichment question effectiveness \u2014 feeds future intakes with better default stakeholder routing."},"event_subscriptions":[{"event_type":"adr.decision.proposed","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { adr_number, title, status: 'Proposed', notion_page_id, stakeholders[] }."},{"event_type":"adr.decision.accepted","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { adr_number, title, status: 'Accepted', notion_page_id, decided_at, review_date }."}],"error_handling":"knowledge base 401/403 \u2192 cache pending ADR in `adr_pending.json`, Messages alert to re-authorize. Validation errors \u2192 fix payload and retry once. Title-similarity hit \u2192 alert + link to existing ADR instead of creating a duplicate. Missing alternatives \u2192 block creation; run enrichment interview.","input_schema":[{"name":"require_alternatives_count","type":"number","default":3,"min":2,"max":10,"description":"Minimum alternatives that must be documented before an ADR can reach Accepted. Enforces thorough evaluation."},{"name":"require_stakeholder_sign_off","type":"boolean","default":true,"description":"When true, ADRs stay in Proposed until every named stakeholder approves via manual_review."},{"name":"documentation_format","type":"enum","options":["adr","rfc","lightweight"],"default":"adr","description":"Full ADR outline, RFC collaborative proposal format, or lightweight (title + context + decision + rationale) for routine decisions."}],"sample_input":{"require_alternatives_count":"{{param.aq_alternatives_count}}","require_stakeholder_sign_off":"{{param.aq_require_sign_off}}","documentation_format":"{{param.aq_doc_format}}"},"tool_hints":["http_request","web_search","file_read","file_write"],"test_fixtures":[],"use_case_flow":{"nodes":[{"id":"i1","type":"start","label":"Manual intake"},{"id":"i2","type":"action","label":"Validate + assign ADR-XXXX"},{"id":"i3","type":"decision","label":"Sufficient context?"},{"id":"i4","type":"action","label":"Enrichment interview (manual_review)"},{"id":"i5","type":"action","label":"Build alternatives matrix"},{"id":"i6","type":"connector","label":"Create knowledge base ADR page","connector":"knowledge_base"},{"id":"i7","type":"action","label":"Stakeholder sign-off (if required)"},{"id":"i8","type":"event","label":"Emit adr.decision.proposed /.accepted"},{"id":"i9","type":"end","label":"ADR documented"}],"edges":[{"source":"i1","target":"i2"},{"source":"i2","target":"i3"},{"source":"i3","target":"i4","variant":"no","label":"Needs enrichment"},{"source":"i3","target":"i5","variant":"yes","label":"Sufficient"},{"source":"i4","target":"i5"},{"source":"i5","target":"i6"},{"source":"i6","target":"i7"},{"source":"i7","target":"i8"},{"source":"i8","target":"i9"}]}}

recipes.name: uc_review_cycle
recipes.description: Periodic review of accepted ADRs: checks whether constraints still hold, presents a review batch with Reaffirm / Re-evaluate / Deprecate options, archives stale deprecated decisions. Default weekly Monday 09:00 — final cadence set at the trigger-composition step.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_review_cycle","title":"Review Cycle","description":"Periodic review of accepted ADRs: checks whether constraints still hold, presents a review batch with Reaffirm / Re-evaluate / Deprecate options, archives stale deprecated decisions. Default weekly Monday 09:00 \u2014 final cadence set at the trigger-composition step.","capability_summary":"Periodic review with Reaffirm / Re-evaluate / Deprecate decisions and auto-archival.","category":"automation","enabled_by_default":true,"execution_mode":"e2e","model_override":null,"suggested_trigger":{"trigger_type":"schedule","config":{"cron":"0 9 * * 1","timezone":"local"},"description":"Default Monday 09:00 \u2014 final cadence set at the trigger-composition step."},"connectors":["knowledge_base","source_control"],"notification_channels":[],"review_policy":{"mode":"always","context":"Every due ADR is presented for human triage (Reaffirm / Re-evaluate / Deprecate). The agent never silently reaffirms."},"memory_policy":{"enabled":true,"context":"Review outcomes per ADR + per category, and time-to-stale patterns \u2014 informs future review-cycle period recommendations."},"event_subscriptions":[{"event_type":"adr.decision.reviewed","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { adr_number, outcome: 'reaffirmed' | 'reevaluate' | 'deprecated', reviewed_at, reviewer_notes }."},{"event_type":"adr.decision.deprecated","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { adr_number, reason, deprecated_at }."},{"event_type":"adr.decision.archived","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { adr_number, deprecated_at, archived_at }."},{"event_type":"adr.decision.stalled","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { adr_number, days_pending }. Emitted when a Proposed ADR has been pending sign-off 14+ days."}],"error_handling":"knowledge base query failure \u2192 retry once at 5s; persistent failure deferred to next cycle. Missing source-control credential \u2192 still run review, skip the code-activity context line. Auto-archive scan uses `auto_archive_after_days` and ignores ADRs still being referenced by other non-archived ADRs.","input_schema":[{"name":"decision_review_period_days","type":"number","default":30,"min":7,"max":365,"description":"Days after acceptance before an ADR surfaces for periodic review."},{"name":"auto_archive_after_days","type":"number","default":365,"min":0,"max":1825,"description":"Days a Deprecated ADR stays active before being archived. 0 disables auto-archival."}],"sample_input":{"decision_review_period_days":"{{param.aq_review_period}}","auto_archive_after_days":"{{param.aq_archive_after}}"},"tool_hints":["http_request","web_search","file_read","file_write"],"test_fixtures":[],"use_case_flow":{"nodes":[{"id":"r1","type":"start","label":"Review tick"},{"id":"r2","type":"connector","label":"Query due ADRs","connector":"knowledge_base"},{"id":"r3","type":"action","label":"Assess context validity (code + web)"},{"id":"r4","type":"action","label":"Present review batch (manual_review)"},{"id":"r5","type":"connector","label":"Patch statuses per outcome","connector":"knowledge_base"},{"id":"r6","type":"connector","label":"Archive deprecated ADRs past threshold","connector":"knowledge_base"},{"id":"r7","type":"event","label":"Emit reviewed / deprecated / archived / stalled"},{"id":"r8","type":"end","label":"Cycle complete"}],"edges":[{"source":"r1","target":"r2"},{"source":"r2","target":"r3"},{"source":"r3","target":"r4"},{"source":"r4","target":"r5"},{"source":"r5","target":"r6"},{"source":"r6","target":"r7"},{"source":"r7","target":"r8"}]}}

recipes.name: uc_command
recipes.description: Webhook-driven entry point: verify operator authorization, parse natural language intent, classify destructive vs safe, execute safe operations directly with formatted Telegram response, or compose a confirmation prompt for destructive operations. Learns team vocabulary over time.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_command","title":"Command Intake & Execution","description":"Webhook-driven entry point: verify operator authorization, parse natural language intent, classify destructive vs safe, execute safe operations directly with formatted Telegram response, or compose a confirmation prompt for destructive operations. Learns team vocabulary over time.","capability_summary":"Natural language command parser with authorization check and destructive-action gating.","category":"operations","enabled_by_default":true,"execution_mode":"e2e","model_override":null,"suggested_trigger":{"trigger_type":"webhook","config":{"source":"telegram","event":"message"},"description":"Telegram Bot API webhook fires on every incoming message. Not user-configurable \u2014 webhook triggers bypass the composition picker."},"connectors":["telegram","devops"],"notification_channels":[],"review_policy":{"mode":"never","context":null},"memory_policy":{"enabled":true,"context":"`learned_aliases` in `ops_state.json` \u2014 recurring phrasing patterns map to canonical operations (e.g., 'bounce the cache' \u2192 'restart Redis')."},"event_subscriptions":[{"event_type":"ops.command.executed","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { operator_id, intent, target, result_status, was_destructive }. Emitted per safe command execution."},{"event_type":"ops.service.unhealthy","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { service, status, latency_ms, error_rate }. Emitted when a health check returns unhealthy/degraded status."}],"error_handling":"Unauthorized sender \u2192 polite rejection + log. Ambiguous intent \u2192 ask clarification. Ops API timeout \u2192 notify + retry + editMessageText. Non-text message \u2192 'text commands only' reply. Duplicate update_id \u2192 skip silently.","input_schema":[{"name":"authorized_users","type":"text","description":"Comma-separated Telegram user IDs allowed to execute commands. Each member can find their ID via @userinfobot."}],"sample_input":{"authorized_users":"{{param.aq_authorized_users}}"},"tool_hints":["http_request","file_read","file_write"],"test_fixtures":[],"use_case_flow":{"nodes":[{"id":"c1","type":"start","label":"Telegram webhook fires"},{"id":"c2","type":"action","label":"Load ops state + check authorization"},{"id":"c3","type":"decision","label":"Authorized?"},{"id":"c4","type":"action","label":"Parse intent + check for pending confirmation"},{"id":"c5","type":"decision","label":"Destructive action?"},{"id":"c6","type":"connector","label":"Execute via ops API","connector":"operations_api"},{"id":"c7","type":"connector","label":"Send confirmation prompt + store pending","connector":"telegram"},{"id":"c8","type":"event","label":"Emit ops.command.executed / ops.service.unhealthy"},{"id":"c9","type":"end","label":"Command handled + logged"},{"id":"c10","type":"error","label":"Reject unauthorized"}],"edges":[{"source":"c1","target":"c2"},{"source":"c2","target":"c3"},{"source":"c3","target":"c4","variant":"yes"},{"source":"c3","target":"c10","variant":"no"},{"source":"c4","target":"c5"},{"source":"c5","target":"c6","variant":"no"},{"source":"c5","target":"c7","variant":"yes"},{"source":"c6","target":"c8"},{"source":"c7","target":"c9"},{"source":"c8","target":"c9"},{"source":"c10","target":"c9"}]}}

recipes.name: uc_confirm
recipes.description: Handles YES/CANCEL replies to pending confirmations: validates the same-operator rule, checks expiry, executes the stored destructive operation, and broadcasts a RED-severity alert to the secondary messaging channel. Webhook-driven on the same Telegram message stream as uc_command.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_confirm","title":"Destructive Action Confirmation","description":"Handles YES/CANCEL replies to pending confirmations: validates the same-operator rule, checks expiry, executes the stored destructive operation, and broadcasts a RED-severity alert to the secondary messaging channel. Webhook-driven on the same Telegram message stream as uc_command.","capability_summary":"Two-step confirmation gate for rollbacks, restarts, and scaling.","category":"operations","enabled_by_default":true,"execution_mode":"e2e","model_override":null,"suggested_trigger":{"trigger_type":"webhook","config":{"source":"telegram","event":"message"},"description":"Fires on the same Telegram webhook as uc_command; routes when message is a YES/CANCEL reply from a user with a pending confirmation."},"connectors":["telegram","devops","messaging"],"notification_channels":[],"review_policy":{"mode":"always","context":"Confirmation IS the review step \u2014 no additional gate needed."},"memory_policy":{"enabled":false,"context":null},"event_subscriptions":[{"event_type":"ops.rollback.executed","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { service, target_version, confirmed_by, rollback_id }. Emitted on confirmed rollback."},{"event_type":"ops.destructive.confirmed","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { operator_id, action_type, target, params }. Emitted for any confirmed restart, scale-down, drain, or config change."}],"error_handling":"Expired confirmation \u2192 auto-cancel + notify 'timed out'. YES from different user \u2192 treat as no-op, keep original pending. Ops API failure during execution \u2192 Telegram notification + retry + emit `persona_error`. Secondary channel down \u2192 queue alert, continue Telegram response.","input_schema":[{"name":"confirmation_timeout_minutes","type":"number","default":5,"min":1,"max":30,"description":"Minutes a pending confirmation stays valid before auto-cancel."}],"sample_input":{"confirmation_timeout_minutes":"{{param.aq_confirmation_timeout}}"},"tool_hints":["http_request","file_read","file_write"],"test_fixtures":[],"use_case_flow":{"nodes":[{"id":"d1","type":"start","label":"YES/CANCEL reply received"},{"id":"d2","type":"action","label":"Load pending confirmation + check expiry"},{"id":"d3","type":"decision","label":"Same user + reply == YES?"},{"id":"d4","type":"connector","label":"Execute destructive operation","connector":"operations_api"},{"id":"d5","type":"connector","label":"Broadcast RED alert to secondary channel","connector":"secondary_messaging"},{"id":"d6","type":"event","label":"Emit ops.rollback.executed / ops.destructive.confirmed"},{"id":"d7","type":"end","label":"Confirmation handled"}],"edges":[{"source":"d1","target":"d2"},{"source":"d2","target":"d3"},{"source":"d3","target":"d4","variant":"yes"},{"source":"d3","target":"d7","variant":"no"},{"source":"d4","target":"d5"},{"source":"d5","target":"d6"},{"source":"d6","target":"d7"}]}}

recipes.name: uc_daily_summary
recipes.description: Compiles a daily rollup of commands executed (by type), current service health, recent deployments, incidents, top operators, and any pending confirmations (should be zero). Posted to Telegram and the secondary messaging channel; archived to `daily_reports/`. Default daily 9 AM — final cadence set at the trigger-composition step.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_daily_summary","title":"Daily Ops Summary","description":"Compiles a daily rollup of commands executed (by type), current service health, recent deployments, incidents, top operators, and any pending confirmations (should be zero). Posted to Telegram and the secondary messaging channel; archived to `daily_reports/`. Default daily 9 AM \u2014 final cadence set at the trigger-composition step.","capability_summary":"Daily rollup of command totals, service health, and deploys posted to both channels.","category":"reporting","enabled_by_default":true,"execution_mode":"e2e","model_override":null,"suggested_trigger":{"trigger_type":"schedule","config":{"cron":"0 9 * * *","timezone":"local"},"description":"Default daily 9 AM \u2014 final cadence set at the trigger-composition step."},"connectors":["telegram","devops","messaging"],"notification_channels":[],"review_policy":{"mode":"never","context":null},"memory_policy":{"enabled":false,"context":null},"event_subscriptions":[{"event_type":"ops.summary.delivered","direction":"emit","description":"Payload: { date, total_commands, services_healthy, services_degraded, deploys }. Emitted per daily run."}],"error_handling":"Ops API unreachable \u2192 partial report from local `command_log.json` with note on stale health data. Secondary channel down \u2192 queue + retry; Telegram delivery proceeds. recipes.name: uc_deletion
recipes.description: Webhook-driven capability. Processes auth provider user.deleted events. Verifies signature, soft-deletes in Supabase (status='churned', deleted_at=NOW()), sends a farewell / feedback email, posts a masked-PII churn alert to the messaging channel, removes local onboarding state. Never hard-deletes. Event-driven — not configurable at the trigger-composition step.

recipes.name: uc_onboarding_drip
recipes.description: Scheduled capability. Reads onboarding state files, advances each user's drip stage by time-since-signup (Day-1 @ 24h, Day-3 @ 72h, Day-7 @ 168h), sends the appropriate email via email, PATCHes Supabase onboarding_step, updates the state file. Default daily 09:00 local — final cadence set at the trigger-composition step.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_onboarding_drip","title":"Onboarding Drip Sequence","description":"Scheduled capability. Reads onboarding state files, advances each user's drip stage by time-since-signup (Day-1 @ 24h, Day-3 @ 72h, Day-7 @ 168h), sends the appropriate email via email, PATCHes Supabase onboarding_step, updates the state file. Default daily 09:00 local \u2014 final cadence set at the trigger-composition step.","capability_summary":"Daily onboarding drip advancement (Day-1 / Day-3 / Day-7 emails).","category":"development","enabled_by_default":true,"execution_mode":"e2e","model_override":null,"suggested_trigger":{"trigger_type":"schedule","config":{"cron":"0 9 * * *"},"description":"Default daily 09:00 local \u2014 final cadence set at the trigger-composition step."},"connectors":["supabase","email"],"notification_channels":[{"type":"email","description":"Drip stage email (Day-1 tips / Day-3 feature spotlight / Day-7 check-in)."}],"review_policy":{"mode":"never","context":null},"memory_policy":{"enabled":true,"context":"Accumulate onboarding completion rate, per-stage drop-off, email timing effectiveness. recipes.name: uc_signup
recipes.description: Webhook-driven capability. Processes auth provider user.created events. Verifies Svix signature, provisions the user in Supabase (idempotent), posts a celebration to the messaging channel, sends a welcome email via email, and initializes the onboarding drip state. Event-driven — not configurable at the trigger-composition step.
recipes.prompt_template: {"id":"uc_signup","title":"User Signup","description":"Webhook-driven capability. Processes auth provider user.created events. Verifies Svix signature, provisions the user in Supabase (idempotent), posts a celebration to the messaging channel, sends a welcome email via email, and initializes the onboarding drip state. Event-driven \u2014 not configurable at the trigger-composition step.","capability_summary":"Signup fan-out across Supabase + messaging + email + local onboarding state.","category":"development","enabled_by_default":true,"execution_mode":"e2e","model_override":null,"suggested_trigger":{"trigger_type":"webhook","config":{"source":"clerk","events":["user.created"],"verify_signature":true},"description":"auth provider webhook on user.created.
